scenarios.name: Specific Performance of Sale Agreement

checks.detail: Specific Relief Act / specific performance cited
At least one statutory section cited
Prayer for execution of sale deed
No invented amounts (10L/50L never in facts)

scenarios.draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE District Court / Civil Court
AT Bengaluru

O.S. No. __________ / 20____

Anita Sharma,
Aged about 52 years,
Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: Indiranagar, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... PLAINTIFF

                    VERSUS

Rajesh Reddy,
Aged about 60 years,
Occupation: {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: HSR Layout, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR SPECIFIC PERFORMANCE OF CONTRACT WITH COSTS

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

JURISDICTION

1. This Court has territorial jurisdiction under Section 20 CPC, as the agreement was entered into at {{PLACE_OF_CONTRACT}}, was to be performed at {{PLACE_OF_PERFORMANCE}}, and the breach occurred at {{PLACE_OF_BREACH}}; in any event, the Defendant resides or carries on business within the local limits of this Court at Bengaluru.

2. This Court has pecuniary jurisdiction to try the present suit, the suit being valued at Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE_WORDS}} Only), which is within the pecuniary limits prescribed for this Court.

LIMITATION

3. The suit is within limitation under Article 54 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, the period prescribed being three years from the date on which the contract was broken. The suit is filed well within the prescribed period.

FACTS OF THE CASE

4. On 10.01.2024, the Plaintiff entered into an Agreement to Sell with the Defendant for the purchase of a residential flat bearing No. 302, 3rd Floor, Sunshine Apartments, Koramangala, Bengaluru, measuring 1200 sq ft, for a total consideration of Rs. 85,00,000/-. The terms of the agreement included the payment of the balance consideration on or before 10.07.2024 against execution of the sale deed. A copy of the Agreement to Sell is annexed hereto as Annexure A.

5. Pursuant to the said agreement, the Plaintiff paid an advance amount of Rs. 25,00,000/- to the Defendant on 10.01.2024 via RTGS. The transaction bears UTR Number HDFC20240110987654. The Plaintiff has retained proof of said payment, which is annexed hereto as Annexure B.

6. The Plaintiff has arranged and kept ready the balance consideration of Rs. 60,00,000/- for payment on the date fixed for execution of the sale deed. The Plaintiff has remained ready and willing to perform her part of the contract since the date of the agreement until the date of filing this plaint.

PARTICULARS OF BREACH

7. On 05.06.2024, the Defendant sent a letter to the Plaintiff expressly refusing to execute the sale deed as agreed. The Defendant stated in said letter that he intended to sell the property to another person who had made a higher offer. A copy of the Defendant's refusal letter is annexed hereto as Annexure C.

8. On 20.06.2024, the Plaintiff issued a legal notice to the Defendant calling upon him to execute the sale deed in accordance with the Agreement to Sell dated 10.01.2024. The notice was sent via post and acknowledged due. A copy of the legal notice and postal receipt is annexed hereto as Annexure D.

9. Despite the receipt of the legal notice and the Plaintiff's readiness to perform, the Defendant has failed and refused to execute the sale deed. The Defendant has not returned the advance amount nor offered to complete the transaction according to the original terms.

LOSS AND DAMAGE

10. By reason of the Defendant's refusal to execute the sale deed, the Plaintiff has been deprived of her rightful entitlement to the property and has incurred costs in pursuing this litigation. The Plaintiff is entitled to recover costs incurred due to the Defendant's breach of contract.

11. The Plaintiff took all reasonable steps to mitigate the loss by issuing a legal notice dated 20.06.2024 calling for specific performance before approaching this Court. The Defendant's refusal forced the Plaintiff to seek judicial intervention.

12. The Plaintiff claims costs of the suit and such other damages as this Court may deem fit to award in the interest of justice, arising directly from the Defendant's failure to honor the Agreement to Sell.

LEGAL BASIS

13. The Plaintiff is entitled to a decree of specific performance of the agreement under Sections 10 and 14 of the Specific Relief Act, 1963, the subject matter being such that the grant of compensation in money would not afford adequate relief.

14. The Plaintiff has been ready and willing to perform his/her part of the agreement at all material times within the meaning of Section 16(c) of the Specific Relief Act, 1963.

CAUSE OF ACTION

15. The cause of action first arose on 05.06.2024 when the Defendant committed default under the agreement and thereby caused the Plaintiff's right to seek specific performance to accrue.

16. The breach was a single event. The Plaintiff's claim for specific performance subsists and remains enforceable in law.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

17. The suit is valued at Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE_WORDS}} Only) for the purposes of jurisdiction and court fee. Court fee on the plaint has been computed at Rs. {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}}/- under the provisions of the Karnataka Court Fees and Suits Valuation Act, 1958 as applicable in the State of Karnataka, with reference to {{COURT_FEE_COMPUTATION_BASIS}} and the applicable rate schedule. The plaint is tendered with the requisite court fee.

PRAYER

In the premises aforesaid, the Plaintiff most respectfully prays that this Hon'ble Court be pleased to:

    (a) Direct the Defendant to execute and register the sale deed in respect of the suit property in favour of the Plaintiff upon receipt of the balance consideration of Rs. {{BALANCE_CONSIDERATION}}/-;
(b) Direct the Defendant to deliver vacant and peaceful possession of the suit property to the Plaintiff (if not already in possession);
(c) In the alternative, award compensation under Section 21 of the Specific Relief Act, 1963 in lieu of or in addition to specific performance, at such amount as this Hon'ble Court deems fit;
(d) Award pendente lite and future interest on the amount paid under Section 34 CPC;
(e) Award costs of the suit to the Plaintiff;
(f) Grant such other and further relief(s) as this Hon'ble Court deems fit and proper;

LIST OF DOCUMENTS

Annexure A — Original agreement dated 10.01.2024

Annexure B — RTGS receipt (UTR: HDFC20240110987654)

Annexure C — Defendant's refusal letter dated 05.06.2024

Annexure D — Legal notice dated 20.06.2024 with postal receipt

VERIFICATION

Verified at {{CITY}} on this the ____ day of __________ 20____.

I, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, the Plaintiff above-named, do hereby verify that the contents of paragraphs 1 to 17 of this plaint are true and correct to my knowledge, and no material facts have been concealed therefrom, and that I have not filed any other suit, petition, or proceeding in any court or before any authority in respect of the same subject matter and cause of action.

DEPONENT

{{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}

PLAINTIFF

                                    THROUGH:
                                    {{ADVOCATE_NAME}}
                                    Advocate for the Plaintiff
                                    Enrollment No. {{ENROLLMENT_NO}}
                                    {{ADVOCATE_ADDRESS}}
                                    Mobile: {{ADVOCATE_PHONE}}
                                    Email: {{ADVOCATE_EMAIL}}

Clarify the above issues and rerun drafting.
